author: @josephlauria-op8if
text:
Hi Robert, Love your videos they are always educational.
I recently came across a video called "I Made a Tree Generate Electricity" please search this to find, I immediately thought that you would really appreciate this, and would try to replicate and share your findings and might even improve it further.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @glencahalin4786
text:
I'd be tempted to make the Sun big enough to take a button battery and some LEDs around the circumference, in the dark it'd show phases of the moon.

I think it should be possible, using a stepper motor and a timer, to advance it one step a day, though it might need careful choosing of the gear ratios.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i like that mate - thanks for sharing

replies.author: @glencahalin4786
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering 
My thoughts were partly from thinking you live near the coast, so, as moon phases also give tidal info, it'd be kind of nice to give it a bit of extra functionality.

Hmm I think over time pushing down on a switch will weaken and break the Sun support mount, if it's left as plastic...So...For a deluxe version it'd be nice to also get rid of the separate Sun battery. Maybe concentric metal disks in the base plate, offset in height with the 2, conductive, outer parts of that support matching different lengths. The idea there is to mechanically stop the Sun support itself from spinning and keep the pos/neg feeds going to the right places
That way an on/off switch could be incorporated somewhere more robust and also it eliminates one battery.

author: @brookestephen
text: The Antikythera machine, found in an ancient shipwreck, could show us how to build an accurate mechanical orrery!  It accurately calculated eclipses, lunar phases, and was a hand-wound gear system.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it is a very cool mechanism for sure

replies.author: @brookestephen
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering so you decided to build it!  Fascinating results!
